world.title: From victim to accused: Ranya Rao's Rs 2 crore fraud twist
world.description: Uniindia: Bengaluru, Mar 23 (UNI) In a striking twist to a high-profile gold smuggling probe, investigators have found that Kannada actress Ranya Rao, an accused in a multi-crore racket, was earlier duped of over Rs 2 crore in a botched up African gold deal, a development that investigators say allegedly shaped the syndicate''s later operations.

science.title: A rare encounter: How Juice came to observe 3I/ATLAS
science.description: No matter how much planning goes into space missions, one must always expect the unexpected. But luck favours the prepared. In July 2025, an unexpected visitor entered our Solar System, interstellar object 3I/ATLAS. ESA’s Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer – Juice – happened to be in the right place, with the right equipment, at the right time to see it. This unique vantage point offered a fleeting opportunity. What followed was a race against time.

science.title: Moby Dick ‘ship sinking’ sperm whales caught headbutting on camera
science.description: New research from the University of St Andrews reports sperm whales headbutting  one another. The behaviour was captured on film and described scientifically for the first time, confirming accounts by 19 th century mariners of sperm whales using their heads to deliberately push and strike objects, occasionally even sinking ships, thereby inspiring Herman Melville’s classic tale Moby Dick.
